caption_gen:
The biopsy shows a dense inflammatory infiltrate within the dermis, primarily composed of lymphocytes and histiocytes. There is also evidence of dermal edema.

Diagnosis: Dermatitis.

The biopsy shows a dermal infiltrate of melanocytic nests and atypical melanocytes. There is also evidence of dermal fibrosis.
Melanoma.
The patient should be evaluated by a dermatologist for excision.

The epidermis shows hyperkeratosis and acanthosis. The dermis contains melanin pigment.
Melanocytic nevus.

The biopsy shows a dermal proliferation of melanocytic nests, some with atypical features, within a background of dermal fibrosis and inflammation.

Melanoma.

The epidermis shows mild acanthosis with focal parakeratosis. The dermis contains a dense infiltrate of lymphocytes, histiocytes, and neutrophils.

Melanoma.

The biopsy shows a dense dermal infiltrate of lymphocytes, predominantly T cells, with some eosinophils and neutrophils. There is also a prominent dermal collagen deposition.
Lymphocytic infiltrate.
Dermatofibroma.

The epidermis shows mild acanthosis. There is a prominent dermal component with numerous melanocytic nests and atypical melanocytes.

Melanoma.

The image shows a dermal infiltrate of lymphocytes, histiocytes, and eosinophils. There is also evidence of dermal edema.
Lymphocytic infiltrate.
Lymphocytic infiltrate.

The image shows a dermal infiltrate of spindle cells, some with elongated nuclei and eosinophilic cytoplasm, arranged in fascicles. There is also a dermal component with features suggestive of fibrosis.

Dermatofibroma.

The epidermis appears intact. The dermis shows a dense infiltrate of melanocytes, some of which are atypical with irregular nuclear contours. There are also scattered brown pigment granules.

Melanoma.
